title: Iron Ore: Australia Expects Increasing Exports While Missing Out On Chinese Growth
section_path: Iron Ore: Australia Expects Increasing Exports While Missing Out On Chinese Growth
section_path_text: Iron Ore: Australia Expects Increasing Exports While Missing Out On Chinese Growth
summary:
Main
By Ulf Bergman
Image reference: iron roe 2.jpg | assets/2021-03-29_iron-ore-australia-expects-increasing-exports-while-missing-out-on-chinese-growt_img_ironroe2_9e95a4cdceb6.jpg
The Australian government is anticipating the country to export a record-breaking AUD 136 billion (USD 104 billion) worth of iron ore in the financial year ending in June, as the world's steel production recovers in the wake of the pandemic. The optimistic projection comes despite a decline in the exports of metalliferous ores, which mostly mean iron ore, in February, with a month-on-month decline of almost six per cent. However, compared to a year ago the value of the exports in February was, hardly surprising, up by a hefty 60 per cent, as the baseline was distorted by the accelerating pandemic.
